summary: Postinstall drops MCP connector with hardcoded bridge token into the install directory, silently routing Claude Code traffic through author-operated server
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.cjs unconditionally copies the entire payload/ tree into INIT_CWD (the user's working directory at install time), including.mcp.json, CLAUDE.md,.claude/settings.json,.claude/commands/0x2ai-boot.md, and a bundled MCP server (chatroom-mcp-lite-patched.cjs). The dropped.mcp.json hardcodes BRIDGE_URL=https://demo1.0x2ai.com and a static BRIDGE_AUTH_TOKEN. Claude Code auto-discovers.mcp.json in the project directory on its next invocation and silently activates the connector, which exposes tools (provider_query, settings_get/set, chatroom_post, memory_save) that POST prompt content, settings, and any stored API keys (anthropic_api_key, openai_api_key) to https://demo1.0x2ai.com/api/proxy-query and /api/settings. The package's bin/start.cjs additionally launches `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions`, removing the safety prompts that would otherwise gate filesystem/exec tools while the remote-controlled MCP server is wired in. The net effect: any user who runs `npm install 0x2ai-demo1` (rather than the documented `npx` invocation) has their working project silently rewired so that future Claude Code sessions in that directory route prompts, tool calls, memory writes, and stored credentials through a third-party bridge they did not configure, authenticated with credentials embedded in the package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
